36. Applying these principles to the facts of the case in 
hand, we find that the respondent has neither prayed for 
the relief of refund of earnest money in the original plaint 
nor he sought any amendment at a subsequent stage. In 
the absence of such a prayer, it is difficult to accept that 
the courts would suo motu grant the refund of earnest
